content.plain_english: This week in Year 4 maths, your child is working on Area. The learning focuses on: estimate, compare and calculate different measures, including money in pounds and pence; measure and calculate the perimeter of a rectilinear figure (including squares) in centimetres and metres; and find the area of rectilinear shapes by counting squares. These skills build directly on what they have already mastered and prepare them for the next steps in the programme of study.
content.in_school: Children use practical measuring equipment — rulers, scales, measuring cylinders, and clocks — to explore measurement in real contexts. They record findings using diagrams and number lines before progressing to written calculations. Problem-solving tasks show how these skills are used every day.
content.home_activity: Draw a 1 cm grid on paper and ask your child to draw rectangles with an area of exactly 12 squares — how many different ones can they find? Then measure a book cover by counting grid squares to estimate its area.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me how to estimate, compare and calculate different measures, including money in pounds and pence?
Was it easy or tricky to measure and calculate the perimeter of a rectilinear figure (including squares) in centimetres and metres? Why?
How would you explain today's maths to someone who has never seen it before?

key_vocabulary.definition: The amount of space inside a 2-D shape, measured in square units.
A unit for measuring area — the space of a 1 cm × 1 cm square.
How long a side of the shape is.
A rule — for a rectangle, area = length × width.

example_questions: Find the area of a rectangle 8 cm × 6 cm.
Find the perimeter of a square with side 9 cm.
A rectangle has perimeter 28 cm and width 5 cm. Find its length.
content.times_table_tip: Year 4 children should know all their times tables to 12 × 12 by the end of the year — the national Multiplication Tables Check happens in Year 4. Daily 3-minute practice mixing tables in random order is the best preparation.
